title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
Is It Advisable To Have ULIP Along with a Term Plan?
Why do you buy a life insurance policy?
Ask yourself this and the first thing that you would think of would be financial security. A life insurance policy covers the risk of premature death and provides financial security to your family members.
What about earning returns on investments?
If you also want returns on investments, you can invest in ULIPs. ULIPs not only allow insurance coverage, but they also help you earn market-linked returns on your investment. The sum assured under a ULIP, however, is usually 10 times the annual premium. Though some plans allow higher multiples, choosing them would incur high mortality charges reducing your effective investment in the capital market. So, if you club ULIPs with term insurance plans, you can get the best of both plans. Let’s understand how.
Benefits of a term insurance plan:
A term insurance plan has the following benefits –
High coverage at affordable premiums
A term insurance plan is meant to secure your finances in case of your premature demise. It is a protection-oriented policy that comes at low premium rates. Since the premiums are low, you can opt for a high sum assured adequate enough to provide for your family’s financial needs. The high sum assured would compensate your family adequately, while the associated premium would be affordable enough to fit into your budget.
Wider coverage through riders
Term life insurance plans allow a range of riders which are additional coverage benefits. Many plans offer both inbuilt and optional riders allowing you to secure your finances against different types of contingencies. These riders enhance the coverage and provide all-around protection.
Financial security
When you buy a term life insurance plan, you know that in your absence, your family will not suffer financially. The death benefit paid by the plan would take care of your family’s lifestyle expenses and goals. Moreover, if you have availed of any loan or debt, the benefit received from the plan can be used to pay off your outstanding liabilities so that they do not fall on your family’s shoulders.
Benefits of ULIPs
ULIPs, on the other hand, offer the following types of benefits –
Attractive returns
ULIPs invest your premium in market-linked securities. There are different types of funds available under ULIPs. You can choose the funds depending on your risk appetite and investment strategy. These funds invest in capital market securities that have the potential to offer attractive returns as the market grows. If you give your investment time, you can earn good returns, which also account for inflation so that you can create an adequate corpus for your goals. You can get an estimate of your returns with the help of a ULIP calculator.
Flexibility
The ULIP plan is a flexible plan that offers you complete control of your investments. You can choose the amount of premium that you want to invest in and the investment funds. If you need funds during the policy tenure, ULIPs allow partial withdrawals after 5 years. If you want to change the investment funds, you can do so through the switching facility. You can also invest additional premiums through top-ups.
Thus, with ULIPs, you get to manage your investment per your needs.
Fulfilment of different financial goals
There are different types of ULIPs available in the market which help in fulfilling the different types of financial goals that you have. If you want to save for your child’s future, you can choose child ULIPs. For retirement planning, you can pick pension ULIPs. If you want to create a corpus for other goals, you can invest in the basic ULIPs. So, with ULIPs, you can create an earmarked corpus for each of your financial goals, and you can check the same with the help of a ULIP calculator.
Tax advantage¹:
While term plans also provide tax benefits, ULIPs go a step further. Besides allowing the 80C tax benefit on the premium, ULIPs also provide tax-free maturity benefits. Your maturity benefit is allowed as a tax-free income under Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act. However, if you buy the ULIP on or after 1st February 2021, the maturity benefit will be tax-free only if your aggregate premium is up to Rs.2.5 lakhs.
Besides the tax advantage on the maturity benefit, you also save taxes when you switch, i.e., change the investment funds. Switching is a tax-free benefit that helps you change your fund preference without incurring any tax liability.
Benefits of buying a term plan and a ULIP:
If you buy a ULIP along with a term plan, you will get the aforementioned benefits of each of the policies. While the term plan would ensure adequate coverage at pocket-friendly premiums, the ULIP would help you create an optimal corpus through market-linked returns on investments. So, clubbing these two plans would take care of two of the basic financial needs – financial security and saving for the future. Conclusion:
So, build yourself an all-around portfolio with both term insurance and ULIP. Get the benefits of both plans and get peace of mind and financial independence.
Frequently asked questions:
Is it worth buying a ULIP along with a Term plan?The purpose of buying a ULIP is very different from that of a term plan. Hence both plans can be combined to create a solution.
Is death benefit for both ULIPs and Term Plans tax-free?Yes, death benefits from both ULIPs and Term Plans are tax-free in the hands of the nominee.
Disclaimer:
In this policy, the investment risk in investment portfolio is borne by the policyholder. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs.The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. full_pdf_text:
Is It Advisable To Have ULIP Along with a Term Plan?
Why do you buy a life insurance policy?
Ask yourself this and the first thing that you might think of would be financial security. A life insurance policy covers the risk of premature death and provides financial security to your family members.
What about earning market linked returns on investments?
If you also want market linked returns on investments, you may consider investing in ULIPs. ULIPs not only allow insurance coverage, but they also help you earn market-linked returns on your investment. So, if you consider purchasing ULIPs with term insurance plans, you can reap the advantages of both plans. Let’s understand how.
Benefits of a term insurance plan:
A term insurance plan has the following benefits –
1. High coverage at affordable premiums
A term insurance plan is meant to secure the financial future of your loved ones in case of your premature demise. It is a protection-oriented policy that comes at affordable premium rates. Since the premiums are affordable, you can opt for a sum assured adequate enough to provide for your family’s financial needs. 2. Wider coverage through riders
Term life insurance plans allows to choose from a range of riders which provide additional coverage benefits with additional nominal premium. Many plans offer both inbuilt and optional riders allowing you to secure your finances against different types of contingencies. These riders enhance the coverage and provide all-around protection. 3. Financial security
When you buy a term life insurance plan, you know that in your absence, your family will not suffer financially. The death benefit paid by the plan would take care of your family’s lifestyle expenses and goals. Moreover, if you have availed of any loan or debt, the benefit received from the plan may be used to pay off your outstanding liabilities so that they do not fall on your family’s shoulders.
Benefits of ULIPs
ULIPs, offer the following types of benefits –
1. Market linked returns
ULIPs invest your premium in market-linked securities. There are different types of funds available under ULIPs. You may choose the funds depending on your risk appetite and investment strategy. These funds invest in capital market securities that have the potential to offer returns associated with risk as the market grows. You can get an estimate of your market linked returns with the help of a ULIP calculator. 2. Flexibility
The ULIP plan is a flexible plan that offers you option to control your investments. You can choose the amount of premium that you want to invest in and the investment funds. If you need funds during the policy tenure, ULIPs allow partial withdrawals after first 5 policy years.
If you want to change the investment funds, you can do so through the switching facility. You can also invest additional premiums through top-up premium. All the aforementioned facilities can be opted subject to terms and conditions of the plan. Thus, with ULIPs, you get to manage your investment per your needs. 3. Fulfilment of different financial goals
There are different types of ULIPs available in the market which help in fulfilling the different types of financial goals that you have. If you want to save for your child’s financial future, you may consider ULIP based child insurance plans. For retirement planning, you may pick pension ULIPs. If you want to create a corpus for other goals, you may consider investing in the basic ULIPs. So, with ULIPs, you can help in creating an earmarked market linked corpus for each of your financial goals, and you can check the same with the help of a ULIP calculator. 4. Tax advantage¹:
Pure term plans provide tax exemption on death benefit and tax deduction on the premiums paid. If you opt for Term plan with Return of Premium benefit, the amount received on policy maturity, is tax-free under Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, subject to satisfaction of conditions stated therein. Besides allowing the 80C tax deduction on the premium and tax-free death benefit, ULIPs also provide tax-free maturity benefits. Your maturity benefit is allowed as a tax-free income under Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act 1961, subject to satisfaction of conditions stated therein. However, if you have purchased the ULIP on or after 1st February 2021, the maturity benefit will be tax-free only if your annual aggregate premium is less than or equal to Rs.2.5 lakhs. Besides the tax advantage on the maturity benefit, you also save taxes when you switch, i.e., change the investment funds. Fund Switching is a tax-free benefit that helps you change your fund preference without incurring any tax liability.
Benefits of buying a term plan and a ULIP:
If you buy a ULIP plan and a term plan, you will get the aforementioned benefits of each of the policies. While the term plan would ensure adequate coverage at pocket-friendly premiums, the ULIP would help you create a corpus through market-linked returns on investments. S Conclusion:
So, consider building yourself an all-around portfolio with both term insurance and ULIP. You may receive the benefits of each plan individually which may help you achieve peace of mind and financial independence.
Frequently asked questions:
1. Is it worth buying a ULIP along with a Term plan?
The purpose of buying a ULIP is very different from that of a term plan. Hence both plans can either be bought together or can be bought separately, depending on the needs of the Insured while purchasing life insurance plan.. Is death benefit for both ULIPs and Term Plans tax-free? Yes, death benefits from both ULIPs and Term Plans are tax-free in the hands of the nominee.
Disclaimer: IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER. Investment in ULIPs is subject to risks associated with the capital markets. The policy holder is solely responsible for his/her decisions while investing in ULIPs. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. complete-picture-is-out/#3333
